<!-- candidate_digest: paste candidate_digest here -->
candidate_digest: 76ad48a1ddb8aa6228d2bce800739cf9c9f1c2476772c07617859fa40a148bac
<!-- domain_outcome: paste domain_outcome here -->
domain_outcome: changes_requested
<!-- gate_claim: paste gate_claim here -->
gate_claim: the receipt diet is correct and finalize-gate-safe: every executed step records a per-step wall-clock duration in the receipt; the diff-scope decision runs all four chains for any edition-touching diff and the claude chain alone only for a genuinely claude-only diff, failing closed to all four on any unresolved base or git error, with the scope decision plus touched-path diff evidence recorded in the receipt; the receipt stays strictly ADDITIVE so the existing chains[] verdict contract (name, exitCode, accepted_red, headSha, workTreeHash) that plan-validator --finalize-check and release-check and the walkthrough/test-release synthetic fixtures read is unchanged; release.js --release-check still requires an unconditional all-four receipt (chains_incomplete on a subset); each hoisted cross-chain repeat runs exactly once in the combined run while every chain stays individually runnable standalone; the #547 code-tree freshness hash still validates a scoped receipt; all four run-chains editions stay in parity (byte-identical claude↔codex, rename-normalized gitlab/gitea) with all four chains green and the measured >=50% common-case wall-clock cut recorded; and the CLAUDE.md/CHANGELOG documentation delta describes only the shipped behavior with no provenance leakage and CLAUDE.md under 200 lines
<!-- gate_surface: paste gate_surface here -->
gate_surface: the full candidate: the run-chains four-edition family (canonical scripts/ copy, the codex byte twin under plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/, the gitlab and gitea rename-normalized ports), its test surfaces (test-run-chains.js and the two forge test ports test-gitlab-run-chains.js / test-gitea-run-chains.js), and the n2 documentation delta (CLAUDE.md Validation Policy + CHANGELOG), reviewed against the accumulated diff vs the claim root base and n1's before/after timing evidence
<!-- gate_aggregation: paste gate_aggregation here -->
gate_aggregation: sequence
upstream_read: n2-docs 500c77477a04

Context echo (from the canonical review context card, read first): plan_schema_version 2, plan_hash 3628c722ce42946dc80f16c00be32532b2f3b9eb65d88cb9a15af702fa7a1813, epoch 1, attempt_ordinal 1, review_phase discovery, gate_mode change_gate, claim_root_base commit f92ec240e3a02c3e8b10d07ebac7e3a9f4a3f088, logical_gate key 40d0c5e30594dda0207de475670204e0c101d74dc047662f63a9f36811de3384, inherited_frontier none, prior_findings none, repair_delta null. Upstream evidence n1-receipt-diet.md (nonce 8176ed345212, RED and GREEN records plus the BEFORE/AFTER timing table) and the user decision record acb-decision.md were both read before review.

Candidate inspected: the accumulated working-tree diff vs f92ec240 (HEAD equals the claim root base; the candidate is uncommitted). Diff confined to the frozen surface: scripts/kaola-workflow-run-chains.js, its codex byte twin, the gitlab/gitea rename-normalized ports, scripts/test-run-chains.js, the two forge test ports, CLAUDE.md, CHANGELOG.md, plus untracked run evidence. No out-of-surface writes.

Clause-by-clause verification (independently re-executed in the worktree, not taken from n1 prose):
- Per-step durations: chains[].steps[] and preamble.steps[] each carry command, duration_ms, exitCode; asserted by T32/G4; canonical suite re-run green here (186 assertions), gitlab port 26, gitea port 26.
- Diff-scope decision: classifyScope fails closed to all four on an unresolved base (T37) and an unresolved diff; edition-coupling paths (plugins/, package.json, forge-chain-referenced scripts, codex-mirrored root scripts) force all four (T34/G6); decision, reason, base, touchedEditionPaths, changedFileCount are recorded in the receipt. But the claude-only branch is not restricted to genuinely claude-only diffs - see finding R1.
- Additive receipt contract: chains[] rows keep name/exitCode/accepted_red/attempts/retried_transient/timed_out/signal; headSha/workTreeHash/codeTreeHash stamping is untouched. The adaptive finalize gate (plan-validator finalize-check arm) is name-agnostic over chains[] with no chains_incomplete in its precedence, so a claude-only subset receipt is tolerated; the code-tree freshness hash is chain-selection-independent and still validates a scoped receipt.
- Release path: run-chains only scope-narrows under --project/--plan with no --chains/--mock; the bare path stays unscoped (T36) and kaola-workflow-release.js reads the bare root .cache/chain-receipt.json; the release-check arm refuses chains_incomplete on any subset and chains_waived on any waiver (plan-validator lines ~3874-3947). Verified unchanged.
- Hoist: a command shared across selected real chains runs exactly once in the preamble (T35 exec-count proof); a preamble failure marks every containing chain red (never a false green); standalone npm run test:kaola-workflow:<edition> is untouched since package.json is parsed read-only. The five current shared validators match n1's list; a claude-only selection hoists nothing.
- Cross-edition parity: cmp proves the codex twin byte-identical; validate-script-sync and edition-sync --check re-run green here; the two forge test ports differ only by forge noun under normalization. n1's recorded four-chain green run covered the code; the docs delta landed after that run, so both contract validators that assert CLAUDE.md content (validate-workflow-contracts, validate-kaola-workflow-contracts) were re-run here - both green; residual staleness fails closed at n4 via the freshness hash.
- Measurement honesty: n1's table is internally consistent (696.5+17.2+79.8+76.8=870.3; cut (870.3-696.5)/870.3=20.0% serial, ~0% concurrent) and recorded in n1 evidence plus CHANGELOG. The >=50% clause is unmet as measured - see finding R3 and the user decision record.
- Docs: CLAUDE.md at 143 lines (under 200); the new Validation Policy bullet and the CHANGELOG entry accurately describe shipped behavior (decision/reason vocabularies, finalize-context predicate, bare-path exemption, honest ~20% figure, no 50% claim); no provenance in the CLAUDE.md bullet; the issue ref in CHANGELOG is its proper home.

Digest preimages (each trigger digest is sha256 of the exact one-line string below, hashed without a trailing newline; producer_evidence_digest is sha256 of the current bytes of n1-receipt-diet.md):
R1 precondition: finalize-context run-chains invocation (--project or --plan, no --chains or --mock-chain) on a diff whose only changes are root files asserted by the codex chain validator but outside plugins/, package.json, forge-chain-referenced scripts, and codex-mirrored scripts/
R1 input: edit only the KW-CLAUDE-TEMPLATE block of commands/workflow-init.md, then run node scripts/kaola-workflow-run-chains.js --project issue-N
R1 expected: all-four chain selection, because the diff is not genuinely claude-only: validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js, which runs only in the codex chain, asserts byte-parity of commands/workflow-init.md with the codex init SKILL
R1 observed: isEditionCouplingPath returns false for commands/workflow-init.md and .agents/plugins/marketplace.json, so classifyScope selects claude-only with reason non_edition_diff, the codex chain is skipped, and the finalize receipt is green where the pre-candidate all-four run would be red
R2 precondition: finalize-context real chain run with KAOLA_RUN_CHAINS_TIMEOUT_MS set, relying on the documented per-chain timeout bound
R2 input: a decomposed multi-step chain whose steps each finish under the configured timeout but whose total wall-clock far exceeds it
R2 expected: the chain is killed once its wall-clock passes KAOLA_RUN_CHAINS_TIMEOUT_MS, per the per-chain contract documented in the run-chains header
R2 observed: runChainSteps passes the full timeout to every step spawn, so the chain runs to completion with timed_out false and the aggregate bound is steps times timeout
R3 precondition: the frozen gate-claim clause requiring a measured >=50% common-case wall-clock cut for the receipt diet
R3 input: n1 instrument-first measurements: claude 696.5s, codex 17.2s, gitlab 79.8s, gitea 76.8s, all-four serial 870.3s
R3 expected: a recorded common-case wall-clock cut of at least 50 percent
R3 observed: a recorded common-case cut of 20.0 percent serial and about 0 percent concurrent, honestly derived and recorded in n1 evidence and CHANGELOG; per the recorded user decision the target is re-attributed to Phase E of the epic

finding: id=R2 scope=in_scope action=fix status=open severity=low fix_role=tdd-guide rationale=timeout-env-contract-now-per-step-while-docs-say-per-chain

verdict: fail
findings_blocking: 2
review_summary: blocking_findings_present
review_attestation: full_review_completed
review_conclusion: Phase B lands B0 step timings, B2 hoisting, and the additive receipt correctly with the release path, finalize freshness, and four-edition parity all verified intact, but the B1 diff-scope classifier under-approximates the codex and forge chains root read surface, so an edit confined to commands/workflow-init.md or .agents/plugins/marketplace.json is misclassified claude-only and produces a false-green finalize receipt where the pre-candidate all-four run would have gone red; that fail-open hole plus the per-step reinterpretation of the documented per-chain timeout must be repaired before this gate can pass, while the honestly measured 20 percent common-case cut stands recorded as a deferred epic-level follow-up per the user decision.
